_meta.title: Dr. Vijay Malik Peaceful Investing — Specification Ledger
_meta.author: Dr. Vijay Malik (Peaceful Investing, drvijaymalik.com)
_meta.purpose: Authoritative source of truth for all vectorized financial parameter checks, SSGR self-funding gate, and cash-quality shields inside Project Beast (Framework 9: fw_malik_peaceful). 5 materialized binary pillar flags drive a 0-5 score.
_meta.provenance_note: Every threshold in this specification is taken verbatim from Dr. Malik's 8-parameter system (Chapter 4 + Chapter 10 Checklist, pp.182-184 of the PDF). Qualitative parameters (shenanigans 5-7: serial acquisitions, smooth earnings, accounting policy changes; Management Tools 1-10) require data not in the StockScan CSV and are intentionally excluded. All calculations run as loop-free, vectorized array masks with conservative NaN handling.

unit_notes.cfo_to_pat: PERCENTAGE — 73.04 = 73% CFO/PAT cash conversion rate (NOT 0.73). Threshold = 70.0 not 0.7.
unit_notes.debt_to_equity: FLOAT RATIO — 0.5 = 0.5x D/E. Malik: strictly ≤ 0.5 preferred.
unit_notes.interest_coverage: FLOAT MULTIPLIER — 3.0 = 3x operating profit / interest expense. Malik: ≥ 3× minimum.
unit_notes.current_ratio: FLOAT RATIO — 1.25 = current assets 1.25× current liabilities. Malik: ≥ 1.25 minimum.
unit_notes.npm: PERCENTAGE — 8.0 = 8% net profit margin. Malik: ≥ 8% for manufacturing; higher for FMCG/services.
unit_notes.ssgr_self_funded: BINARY FLAG — 1 = SSGR ≥ actual sales growth rate (self-funded). 0 = debt-dependent growth.

pillar_g_growth_runway._book_reference: Chapter 4 (Parameter 1), Chapter 10 Checklist item 1, Chapter 15 Quick-Reference: 'Sales Growth 10Y CAGR >10% (>15% preferred). Must be organic — exclude M&A-driven jumps. Year-by-year consistency matters.'

growth_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — NaN 10Y revenue CAGR filled from 5Y CAGR; both NaN → 0 < 10 → gate fails (cannot verify growth runway)
growth_gate.rationale: Malik's Parameter 1 identifies a 10% revenue CAGR as the floor for a self-funding compounder. Below 10%, the company's SSGR is unlikely to cover its capital needs even with high margins. 15% is preferred — companies like Asian Paints (20%+ CAGR), HDFC Bank (18%+), Pidilite (15%+) all clear the floor with headroom. The 10% floor corresponds to Malik's Scenario A (SSGR > Sales Growth) in the SSGR framework (Chapter 2).

pillar_p_profit_stability._book_reference: Chapter 4 (Parameter 2): 'Minimum 8% for manufacturing, higher for services/FMCG. Must be stable or improving. Declining NPM = moat erosion.' Chapter 10 Checklist item 2: 'NPM >8%, stable or improving.'
pillar_p_profit_stability._display_label: Pricing Power Shield: Stable NPM Profile ≥ 8%

margin_gate.fillna_strategy: npm.fillna(0) — NaN current NPM → 0 < 8 → gate fails. npm_1yb.isna() — NaN prior year treated as 'data unavailable', not as failure (conservative: don't penalize newer listings for missing YoY data).
margin_gate.rationale: Malik's Parameter 2 checks BOTH level (≥8%) AND stability (must be stable or improving). A one-year NPM spike from an exceptional quarter does not reflect the business's true earning power. The dual check — current ≥8% AND (prior year ≥6% OR unavailable) — prevents a spike from masquerading as stable pricing power. The 6% prior-year floor is a 25% haircut on the 8% current threshold, allowing for normal business volatility without accepting structural margin erosion.

pillar_f_debt_fortress._book_reference: Chapter 4 (Parameters 4, 5, 6): 'Interest Coverage ≥ 3×. D/E < 0.5 preferred. Current Ratio ≥ 1.25 minimum.' Chapter 10 Checklist items 4, 5, 6. Chapter 5 (Layer 2 Balance Sheet MoS): 'D/E < 0.5 + CR > 1.5 + Interest Coverage > 5× = high balance sheet MoS.'
pillar_f_debt_fortress._display_label: Leverage Cushion Gate: ICR ≥ 3×, D/E ≤ 0.5, CR ≥ 1.25

interest_coverage_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — NaN interest coverage for non-financial, non-debt-free companies → 0 < 3 → gate fails. Financial sector fully exempt.
interest_coverage_gate.rationale: Malik's minimum: 3× operating profit / interest expense. Below 3× = debt stress. A company that earns only 2× its interest charges is dangerously dependent on continued access to credit. The 3× floor provides a cushion: a 33% decline in operating profit still leaves the company able to service debt.

debt_to_equity_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(999) — NaN D/E → 999 >> 0.5 → gate fails (unknown leverage is never a safe assumption).
debt_to_equity_gate.rationale: Malik's stricter standard vs industry peers. Below 0.5× = business grows without significant external capital. At D/E 0.5, equity finances two-thirds of assets — strong business MoS. FDC Limited, Container Corporation of India: both had D/E near zero when operating at SSGR surplus.

current_ratio_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — NaN current ratio → 0 < 1.25 → gate fails.
current_ratio_gate.rationale: Malik's minimum liquidity floor. Current ratio < 1.0 = company cannot meet short-term obligations from current assets alone — operational liquidity crisis. The 1.25 floor provides a 25% buffer: even if 20% of current assets are non-realizable, the company can still cover current liabilities.

financial_sector_exemption.behaviour: Financial sector companies (banks, NBFCs, insurance) are FULLY EXEMPT from all three sub-gates (ICR, D/E, CR). Rationale: (a) interest_coverage is structurally inapplicable — banks earn interest, not pay it; (b) D/E > 1.0 is structurally normal for leveraged financial businesses; (c) current_ratio is meaningless for institutions with liquid asset portfolios. This mirrors Coffee Can and QGLP financial sector exemptions.

pillar_c_cash_generation._book_reference: Chapter 4 (Parameter 7 + Parameter 8): 'CFO positive every year. cCFO ≈ cPAT: over 10 years, total operating cash ≈ total reported profit.' Chapter 3 (FCF): 'PAT can be manufactured through accounting. Cash cannot.' Chapter 6 (Shenanigan 1): 'cCFO < cPAT = master red flag.'
pillar_c_cash_generation._display_label: Audited Reality Floor: Cash Conversion CFO/PAT ≥ 70%

cash_generation_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — NaN CFO/PAT → 0 < 70 → gate fails (missing cash flow data = cannot verify earnings quality).
cash_generation_gate.rationale: Malik's Parameter 8 (cCFO vs cPAT) requires that cumulative operating cash flow tracks cumulative reported profit over 10 years. The single-year proxy CFO/PAT ≥ 70% captures this requirement: if cash from operations is at least 70% of reported profit in any year, the earnings are substantively cash-backed. The 70% threshold matches Malik's '⚠️ MONITOR: cCFO = 70–100% of cPAT' zone — the minimum acceptable cash backing.
cash_generation_gate.unit_warning: CRITICAL: cfo_to_pat in CSV is PERCENTAGE (e.g. 73.04 = 73%). Threshold must be 70.0 not 0.7.
cash_generation_gate.threshold_hierarchy: In this system: Parikh=75% > Malik=70% floor. Malik's 70% is the minimum acceptable proxy for his cCFO ≥ cPAT condition. Values 50-70% = Malik's red flag zone; below 50% = sell alert.

pillar_s_self_funded._book_reference: Chapter 2 (SSGR Framework): 'SSGR > Sales Growth Rate: THE GOLD STANDARD. The company can fund all its growth from internal profits. It doesn't need debt.' Chapter 5 (Business MoS): 'SSGR > Sales Growth + FCF% > 25% = high business MoS.'
pillar_s_self_funded._display_label: Sustainable Growth Core: SSGR ≥ Actual Sales Growth Rate

ssgr_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — NaN ssgr_self_funded → 0 → gate fails (cannot verify self-funding; conservative).
ssgr_gate.rationale: SSGR self-funding is Malik's most original contribution to Indian investing — his 'signature signal'. When SSGR (= NFAT × NPM × (1-DPR) - Dep) exceeds the company's actual sales growth rate, the entire growth is internally funded — no debt needed. FDC Limited: SSGR 30-40%, growth 8-10% → zero debt. Container Corporation: SSGR 22-25%, growth 8-11% → debt-free cash surplus. The inverse — Amtek India (SSGR ~0%, growth 25-30%) — resulted in 33× debt expansion and eventual collapse.
ssgr_gate.formula_reference: SSGR = NFAT × NPM × (1 − DPR) − Dep (using 3-year averages of all inputs per Chapter 2)

pass_flag_logic._logic: AND of all 5 materialized pillar flags: malik_growth_runway & malik_profit_stability & malik_debt_fortress & malik_cash_generation & malik_self_funded
pass_flag_logic._description: 1 = confirmed Vijay Malik Peaceful Investor screen pass: sales growing, margins stable, fortress balance sheet, cash-backed earnings, SSGR self-funded. 0 = one or more gates failed.

score_column_logic._description: Sum of all 5 pillar flags. Score=5 identical to malik_pass=1. Score=4 = one gate blocking. Enables near-miss ranking of Peaceful Investing compliance.
score_column_logic._use_case: Sort by malik_score descending to rank companies approaching the full Vijay Malik standard. A score of 4/5 with only the SSGR gate missing is materially different from a 4/5 missing the cash generation gate.

vectorization_matrix.description: All 5 gates are pure vectorized Pandas/NumPy boolean masks. Zero loops, zero apply(), zero iterrows(). NaN strategy is conservative: missing data defaults to gate failure for all numeric gates.

nan_handling.rev_gr_10y: fillna from rev_gr_5y; both NaN → fillna(0) → 0 < 10 → gate fails
nan_handling.npm_1yb: isna() treated as 'unavailable' — does NOT fail the gate (conservative: don't penalize missing prior year)
nan_handling.interest_coverage: fillna(0) → 0 < 3 → gate fails (financial sector fully exempt)
nan_handling.debt_to_equity: fillna(999) → 999 > 0.5 → gate fails (unknown leverage never safe)
nan_handling.cfo_to_pat: fillna(0) → 0 < 70 → gate fails (0% cash backing = gate correctly fails)

not_implementable.reason: Malik's master forensic signal (Parameter 8, Shenanigan 1) requires 10 years of annual CFO data. StockScan CSV provides only the current year's cfo_to_pat ratio (a single-year proxy). The single-year proxy is materially weaker but is the only implementable gate.
No M&A history in StockScan CSV. Cannot detect whether revenue growth is organic or acquisition-driven.
Requires year-by-year annual PAT series for 5+ years. Only current year and 1-year-back data available in CSV.
Cannot detect accounting policy changes from a financial ratio CSV — requires reading Notes to Accounts in annual reports.

The system's existing tax_rate_est = (EBITDA - PAT) / EBITDA = EBITDA gap, not effective tax rate. The tax check is partially proxied by rf_tax_panic in forensic_engine.py (effective tax < 10%). Adding a second tax gate here would overlap without adding information.

10-year cumulative FCF calculation requires annual FCF series. CSV provides only current year FCF. The SSGR self-funded gate (Pillar S) captures the conceptually equivalent signal — if SSGR covers growth, the company by definition generates surplus FCF.

Background check, salary discipline, RPT analysis, warrant history, succession planning, credit rating direction — all require qualitative data sources (SEBI orders, annual reports, credit agency reports) not in CSV.

malik_growth_runway.interpretation: 1 = revenue CAGR (10Y or 5Y) >= 10%; self-funding growth engine confirmed.

malik_profit_stability.interpretation: 1 = NPM >= 8% stable (not a one-year spike); pricing power confirmed.

malik_debt_fortress.interpretation: 1 = fortress balance sheet: ICR >= 3x AND D/E <= 0.5 AND CR >= 1.25 (financial sector exempt).

malik_cash_generation.interpretation: 1 = CFO/PAT >= 70%; reported earnings are substantively backed by real operating cash.

malik_self_funded.interpretation: 1 = SSGR >= actual sales growth; growth is internally self-funded without debt.

malik_pass.interpretation: 1 = all 5 pillar gates cleared; confirmed Vijay Malik Peaceful Investor screen pass.

malik_score.interpretation: 0-5 count of pillar gates cleared. 5 = identical to malik_pass=1.

vs_coffee_can.focus: Coffee Can (Mukherjea): ROE >= 15% sustained compounders. Malik: SSGR self-funding as the primary structural filter — a company passes regardless of ROE level if it can fund growth internally.
vs_coffee_can.ssgr_unique: SSGR self-funding gate is UNIQUE to Malik across all 32 frameworks in this system.
vs_coffee_can.cash_quality: Malik: CFO/PAT >= 70% (lower floor). Coffee Can: CFO/EBITDA >= 90% (different denominator, higher bar on EBITDA basis).

vs_diamond.debt: Diamond: D/E < 0.5 (same threshold, different framework). Malik: D/E <= 0.5 + CR >= 1.25 + ICR >= 3x — a 3-factor debt fortress vs Diamond's single D/E gate.
vs_diamond.ssgr: Diamond has no SSGR gate. Malik's signature signal is absent from Diamond.

vs_malik_in_scoring_mode.note: The Malik composite 0-100 score (malik_score) in the QUALITY scoring section measures historical performance percentile rank. The fw_malik_peaceful binary framework is a separate pass/fail screen — it is possible to have a high malik_score composite but FAIL fw_malik_peaceful (e.g. SSGR deficit stocks with excellent historical ROCE).
